stemHtml:
A twin-engine piston aeroplane has two wing tanks with a capacity of 250 litres each and a centre tank with a capacity of 600 litres. A fuel pump ensures that the wing tanks are fed from the centre tank at a rate that they are always full. Fuel consumption is 125 litres/h per engine.

You depart with 700 litres of fuel on board. 35 minutes into the flight, the fuel pump feeding the wing tanks from the centre tank fails. What is your endurance?
